macro_summary: Markets closed Friday largely flat — SPY +0.03% to $679.46, QQQ +0.27% to $611.07 — but the macro picture shifted dramatically over the weekend. Per Yahoo Finance and Investor's Business Daily, US-Iran peace talks collapsed and the US Navy will blockade Iranian ports starting Monday, driving Dow and S&P 500 futures sharply lower ahead of this week's open. WTI surged 8.64% to $104.91 on Friday, reflecting the return of Hormuz closure risk that had briefly appeared to be resolving; the situation is now actively re-escalating, entering Day 36+ of the broader crisis. VIX fell 1.28% to 19.23 on Friday's close but futures weakness pre-open suggests this will reverse sharply Monday. The MAS Monetary Policy Statement is due Monday April 14 at 8pm ET — a HIGH-impact event for Singapore bank positioning.
commodities_note: WTI's 8.64% single-session surge to $104.91 is the dominant commodity signal — this is not a data anomaly but a genuine repricing as blockade fears replace ceasefire optimism. The macro news confirms the US Navy blockade announcement over the weekend, which will likely push WTI higher at Monday's open. Gold slipped 0.68% to $4,738.50, giving back some of its safe-haven premium as some risk was priced in earlier in the week; however, with escalation resuming, gold likely re-bids Monday. DXY ticked up 0.33% to 99.03, consistent with flight-to-safety demand. US10Y at 4.32% (up 0.56%) continues to hold above the 4.20% NIM-pressure threshold for Singapore banks. OXY and energy ETFs (XLE) merit watching given WTI's re-escalation — WTI is up meaningfully from the week's lows and the blockade removes any near-term supply relief narrative.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

D05_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at WATCH. DBS sits 1.5% above its 50-day average of SGD 56.46, with RSI at 60.7 — technically acceptable but right at the upper edge of the comfortable entry zone. The tight invalidation (SGD 56.46, only 1.5% below) warrants noting: the nearest support used for the R:R calculation is very close, so use the 200-day average at SGD 52.43 (8.5% below) as the mental stop for position sizing. Nearest resistance is SGD 57.47 (only 0.3% above, tight_upside applies), so the R:R uses SGD 58.78 as the meaningful ceiling — producing a 1.7:1 ratio, which is below the 2.0 threshold for ACCUMULATE. The thesis structural headwind remains: US10Y at 4.32% continues above the 4.20% threshold where NIM compression eases for Singapore banks, and analysts expect earnings to decline 8.8% — a notable negative. The MAS Monetary Policy Statement due April 14 is a binary event that could move the cluster; entering before this resolves adds unnecessary risk. Volume is below average, cautioning against a full position. Forward PE of 13.9x (10.3% premium to cluster median of 12.6x) is not a bargain given negative revenue growth of -3.3%. Bottom line: DBS offers a reasonable 5.3% dividend yield, but the NIM headwind and Monday's MAS statement argue for waiting — look for a pullback toward SGD 56.50 or a clean MAS catalyst before entering.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

O39_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION (hard block: RSI at 65.3 exceeds the 65 hard ceiling, entry blocked regardless of other factors). OCBC has surged 8.2% in a month, driven partly by an analyst upgrade and its role in channeling SGD 600 million of client inflows into the LionGlobal Singapore Trust Fund, per Simply Wall St. At 4.76% above its rising 50-day average, RSI overbought, and with R:R only 1.1:1 (derived quality, not observed swing points), the setup is unfavourable for fresh entry. Revenue growth of 6.5% and forward PE at cluster median (12.6x) are reasonable, but the PEG of 3.81 and earnings growth of only 2.7% mean you're paying in full for modest growth. The 200-day average at SGD 18.64 is 17% below current price — structural support is far away if sentiment shifts on Monday's MAS statement. Bottom line: OCBC's recent strength is real but the entry gate is firmly closed at current levels — revisit if RSI cools below 58 or price pulls back toward SGD 21.50.

U11_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. UOB is sitting just below its flat 50-day average of SGD 37.30 (currently -0.31% below), with RSI at 54.2 — a textbook neutral setup with no compelling entry or exit signal. The nearest resistance is SGD 37.40 (0.6% above, tight_upside applies), so the R:R calculation uses SGD 39.49 as the meaningful ceiling, producing a 1.6:1 ratio — below ACCUMULATE threshold. Invalidation is the 200-day average at SGD 35.69 (4.0% below), which is the nearest structural support. Analyst consensus is HOLD with -6.7% expected earnings growth — forward PE at 10.3x (18.3% discount to cluster median) prices in some weakness, but negative revenue growth of -1.8% limits upside conviction. The MAS statement Monday is the key catalyst. Bottom line: UOB is the cheapest of the three Singapore banks on PE, but declining earnings and the NIM headwind mean patience is warranted — upgrades to WATCH if Monday's MAS statement signals rate sensitivity easing or price reclaims SGD 37.30 with volume.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

NVDA.signal_rationale: Signal changed from BUY to WATCH — the R:R ratio has deteriorated to 0.9:1 (unfavourable), with tight_upside triggered because the nearest resistance at $189.10 is only 0.2% above current price, making the pipeline automatically use $194.67 as the meaningful ceiling; even then, R:R to $194.67 from $188.63 is only 3.2% upside vs. 3.5% to invalidation at $182.07 (50-day SMA). Fragility gate also applies: the Blackwell Ultra ramp remains the sole load-bearing leg — multi-day price confirmation is accumulating (2 days above 50-day SMA) but the blockade escalation overnight introduces fresh macro risk that could reset the tape. NVDA is up 6.3% over 5 days and 3.6% above its 50-day SMA of $182.07; RSI at 61.4 is not overbought but volume is below average — enter smaller or wait. The Blackwell Ultra ramp thesis is intact: Q4 FY2026 revenue of $68.1B (+73% YoY) per NVDA earnings, and the Q1 FY2027 guide of $78B implies Blackwell is shipping ahead of schedule. Forward PE of 17x with PEG 0.77 and 73% revenue growth is genuinely cheap on growth-adjusted basis. FCF yield of 1.27% is thin. Invalidation at $182.07 (50-day SMA); structural support at $177.41 (4.2% below via 200-day SMA). Bottom line: NVDA is reasonably priced for its growth rate, but Monday's Iran blockade news creates a binary open — wait for the dust to settle and confirm the 50-day SMA holds before entering.

AMD.entry_block: BLOCKED: +17.1% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: days_above_sma50=6 (<30), sma50 not rising, RSI 70 (>=55).

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

AMD.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. AMD has surged 17.1% above its 50-day SMA of $209.35 — hard blocked from entry. RSI at 70.1 is overbought, the 5-day gain of 12.7% triggered the momentum-chase block, and R:R is only 0.6:1 (downside to 50-day SMA is 14.6% vs. 8.5% upside to $265.85 resistance). Per AMD's IR press release (February 24, 2026), the Meta GPU supply framework for MI450 (up to 6GW, first 1GW shipments H2 2026) validates AMD as a credible alternative to NVDA at hyperscale — this is a framework agreement, not a firm purchase order, and revenue is contingent on MI450 benchmark performance. Analyst consensus median price target of $300 per Insider Monkey, but 45 of 56 analysts already have Buy ratings — much of the optimism is priced in. PEG of 0.73 with 34% revenue growth suggests reasonable value at the right entry price, but not 17% extended. The 50-day SMA at $209.35 and 200-day at $199.41 are the retracement targets to watch. Bottom line: AMD's growth story is real, but at current levels you're chasing — wait for a pullback toward $215-220 before adding this to the active watch list.

INTC.entry_block: BLOCKED: +32.1% above 50-day SMA (>20% extreme extension). No trend exception applies at this level — entry requires a meaningful pullback regardless of trend duration.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

INTC.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. Intel is 32.1% above its 50-day SMA of $47.21 — extreme extension, hard blocked, no trend exception applies at this level. RSI at 75.4 is deep in overbought territory. The CHIPS Act-driven move (23.8% in 5 days) has been rapid but the thesis requires scrutiny: the 18A foundry node has NO confirmed external customers as of April 2026 — internal products (Panther Lake) only. The forward PE of 61.4x is 182.9% premium to the Semis cluster median of 21.7x, negative FCF yield of -1.44% means Intel is cash-consuming, and revenue growth is -4.1%. R:R is 0.2:1 — deeply unfavourable. Invalidation at the 50-day SMA would require a 24.3% decline; structural support at $40.63 is 34.9% below. Intel reports earnings April 24 (11 days away) — a binary event that will test whether the foundry thesis has any near-term revenue. Bottom line: Intel's CHIPS Act deal is the entire thesis here, but the technicals are screaming overextension — the earliest sensible entry point is after earnings at $47-52, conditional on foundry customer wins being confirmed.

ASML.entry_block: BLOCKED: +6.2% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: days_above_sma50=3 (<30), sma50 not rising, RSI 61 (>=55).

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

ASML.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION (hard block: 6.2% above 50-day SMA, RSI 61.4, days above SMA only 3 — trend exception not met). ASML earnings are April 15 — two trading days away — making this a binary event that blocks any entry regardless. The Japan Rapidus subsidy news (per Simply Wall St.) confirms ASML's centrality to high-end chipmaking, and the High-NA EUV ramp for Intel 14A in H2 2026 per ASML's Q4 2025 earnings call remains the multi-year demand driver. At 12.7% recovery over 7 days, the price has moved sharply ahead of the print. PEG of 2.43 is expensive for growth; FCF yield of 1.87% is thin. Nearest resistance is $1,491.51 (0.9% above, tight_upside applies), so R:R uses $1,547.22 as the ceiling — producing 0.8:1, unfavourable. Structural support at $1,283.60 (13.2% below) is the key downside level if earnings disappoint. Bottom line: ASML is the critical infrastructure play for AI chip manufacturing, but with earnings in 2 days and price 6% extended above its average, this is a hold-and-watch situation — revisit after the April 15 print for a cleaner entry.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

MU.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at WATCH. Micron is blocked from entry by the 5-day momentum rule (+14.8% in 5 days exceeds the 10% chase threshold), but the underlying thesis is strengthening. KeyBanc Capital Markets reiterated Overweight with a $600 price target on April 6 (per Insider Monkey), citing structural shifts in how Micron contracts with hyperscalers. Revenue growth of 196.3% and analyst consensus of 756% earnings growth are extraordinary — though the forward PE of 4.3x vs. trailing PE of 19.8x signals an expected earnings inflection that deserves scrutiny rather than blind acceptance. PEG of 0.24 appears anomalous given the forward PE — verify whether the earnings growth estimate is annualized appropriately; treat cautiously. FCF yield of 0.61% is thin, meaning the thesis depends on future earnings materializing. Invalidation at $402.81 (50-day SMA, 4.2% below); R:R to $437.65 is 1.0:1 — barely neutral. The HBM demand story is inference-driven (KV cache scaling), not training — this distinction matters for sustainability. Micron's CEO's bullish outlook on memory prices should be taken as a seller's perspective. Bottom line: Micron is a compelling growth story at a low multiple, but the recent 14.8% 5-day surge means you've missed the clean entry — wait for consolidation back toward $405-415 before adding.

TSM.entry_block: BLOCKED: +5.9% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: days_above_sma50=3 (<30), RSI 63 (>=55).

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

TSM.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION (hard block: 5.9% above 50-day SMA, RSI 62.7, only 3 days above SMA). TSMC's Q1 2026 preliminary results showed record revenue, up 35% year-on-year per Simply Wall St., beating expectations — and earnings are April 16 (3 days away), making this a binary event that argues strongly for patience. CoWoS capacity is fully booked, scaling from ~80K to 120-130K wafers/month by end-2026, with NVDA booked for 800-850K wafers (>50% capacity). Forward PE of 20.1x with 20.5% revenue growth is reasonable (PEG 1.3, in-line). R:R at current levels is 0.4:1 — deeply unfavourable given 5.6% downside to 50-day SMA vs. 2.2% upside to first resistance. Structural support at $315.99 (14.7% below) is the major downside reference if the post-earnings move is negative. Bottom line: TSMC is the kingmaker of the AI buildout with confirmed record Q1 revenue, but with earnings in 3 days and the stock already extended, waiting for the April 16 print is the disciplined move — a post-earnings pullback to $350-360 would be the ideal entry.

AMZN.entry_block: BLOCKED: +11.7% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: days_above_sma50=3 (<30), sma50 not rising, RSI 72 (>=55).

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

AMZN.signal_rationale: Signal changed from CAUTION to HOLD — AMZN has recovered 11.7% above its 50-day SMA, RSI reached 71.5 (overbought), and the prior CAUTION signal was based on deteriorating technicals that have now sharply reversed; HOLD reflects the fact that the stock is extended and not actionable at current levels, but the thesis is intact with no fundamental deterioration. Per Simply Wall St., Amazon disclosed a $20B+ revenue run rate from in-house AI chips (Graviton/Trainium) in its annual shareholder letter, with triple-digit year-on-year growth and plans to potentially sell these chips externally — plus ~$200B in 2026 capital expenditures supported by large AWS customer commitments. The stock is hard blocked at 11.7% above SMA50 with RSI 71.5 and volume above average (volume confirms the overbought move, not a buy signal here). R:R is 0.4:1 — unfavourable, with 10.5% downside to SMA50 vs. 4.2% upside. Forward PE of 25.4x with 13.6% revenue growth produces a PEG of 1.83 — modestly expensive. Analyst consensus expects only 5% earnings growth — well below the PE multiple, a valuation concern. Structural support at $197.66 (17.1% below) is the major downside level. Bottom line: Amazon's AI chip business is emerging as a genuine new revenue stream, but after a 13.6% 5-day surge the stock needs to consolidate — wait for a pullback toward $213-220 (50-day SMA zone) before considering entry.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

GOOG.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at WATCH. Alphabet sits 2.59% above its 50-day SMA of $307.75 — within the 2-5% slightly extended zone — with RSI at 62.1 and below-average volume. Nearest resistance is $319.23 (1.1% above, tight_upside applies), so R:R uses $328.22 as the ceiling, producing 1.6:1 — below ACCUMULATE threshold. Invalidation at $307.75 (50-day SMA, 2.5% below); structural support at $295.95 (6.3% below) is the meaningful downside reference. Needham reiterated Buy with a $400 price target on April 8 per Insider Monkey, citing AI positioning. TPU v7 is being rented by Meta, Anthropic, and Apple — the Meta multi-year deal (per The Information, February 2026) validates commercial demand outside Google Cloud, which is the cleanest demand signal. The Catalyst Alert linking Alphabet to Broadcom (per Motley Fool) reflects AVGO's custom ASIC work on TPU chips — cross-reference TSM capacity for fab constraints. PEG of 1.86 with 18% revenue growth is neutral — not cheap, not expensive. FCF yield of 1.0% is thin. Earnings April 30 (17 days out). Bottom line: Google is fairly priced for what you're getting, and the TPU commercial traction story is credible — but price needs to hold the 50-day SMA on any Monday selloff before this becomes a clean entry.

MSFT.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Microsoft is 5.78% below its declining 50-day SMA of $393.62, with the 100-day at $435.83 and 200-day at $472.59 — price is well below all key averages, confirming sustained downtrend. RSI at 38.4 is approaching oversold territory but not yet there. This is caution_source=base_scorer, making ACCUMULATE technically evaluable — but it fails two gates: (1) R:R of 7.2:1 using the $488.02 resistance target appears highly distorted (31.6% upside from $370.87 with only 4.4% downside to $354.62 support) — while rr_distorted is flagged false, a 7.2:1 ratio from a 4.4% stop to a 31.6% target spanning across multiple SMAs in a declining trend is not a realistic near-term setup; (2) the downward trend in all SMAs makes the invalidation at $354.62 (4.4% below) a credible near-term risk. Goldman Sachs maintained Buy on April 6 per Insider Monkey. OpenAI concentration risk remains: ~45% of MSFT's commercial RPO is tied to OpenAI, making Azure AI growth partially circular (MSFT investment → OpenAI → Azure credits → revenue). Forward PE of 19.7x with PEG 1.2 is actually reasonable for a MSFT in downtrend — the 19.3% discount to BigTech cluster median is the value argument. Revenue growth 16.7% with 59.8% expected earnings growth would be bullish if confirmed. Earnings April 30. Bottom line: Microsoft is getting cheaper, and the fundamentals are reasonable — but the declining trend across all three moving averages means patient investors should wait for a technical floor to form around $354-360 before entering.

AVGO.entry_block: BLOCKED: +13.9% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: days_above_sma50=4 (<30), sma50 not rising, RSI 71 (>=55).

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

AVGO.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. Broadcom has surged 18.1% in 5 days to 13.9% above its 50-day SMA — hard blocked from entry at any level. RSI at 71.0 is overbought, volume is above average (confirming the overbought extension, not a buy signal), and R:R is only 0.3:1 — the nearest resistance at $372.10 is 0.1% above current price (tight_upside), making the meaningful ceiling $384.93 (3.6% upside) vs. 12.2% downside to SMA50. Per Motley Fool, Alphabet reportedly made Broadcom's stock a must-buy — likely referencing Broadcom's dominant position in custom ASIC design for Google TPU. Broadcom's Q1 FY2026 record revenue of $19.3 billion with AI semiconductor revenue nearly doubling year-on-year per TheStreet validates the thesis. PEG of 0.81 with 29.5% revenue growth is genuinely attractive — the custom ASIC story (Google TPU, Meta, OpenAI inference) is real. Structural support at $322.03 (13.3% below) is the major downside level. Do not conflate AVGO's custom ASIC model with NVDA merchant GPUs — they are competitive alternatives. Earnings June 4 (52 days out). Bottom line: Broadcom's AI chip business is generating real results, but after an 18% week the stock needs a significant pullback to $330-340 before offering a clean entry.

LITE.entry_block: BLOCKED: +36.2% above 50-day SMA (>20% extreme extension). No trend exception applies at this level — entry requires a meaningful pullback regardless of trend duration.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

LITE.sma50_warning: SMA50 is 27% below price — pullback to SMA50 would require a crash-level move. Use shorter-term support levels for entry guidance instead.
LITE.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION (hard block: 36.2% above 50-day SMA — extreme extension, no trend exception at this level regardless of the 201-day streak above SMA50). The NVDA $2B strategic investment confirmed via NVIDIA's investor relations press release (March 2, 2026) is real and transformative — a multiyear nonexclusive partnership with multibillion purchase commitments for AI optics as optical interconnects become the bandwidth bottleneck for GPU clusters beyond 100K GPUs. S&P 500 inclusion (March 9, 2026) is the primary driver of the current extension; per the learned heuristics, inclusion buying typically exhausts within 3-5 sessions of the effective date. Per the sma50_warning, the 50-day SMA at $658.87 is 27% below price — a pullback to that level would be a crash-level move. Use the shorter-term support at $642.37 (28.4% below) for entry guidance. Forward PE of 56.7x is 161.3% premium to Semis cluster median of 21.7x — expensive, but PEG of 0.16 appears anomalous (flag: PEG appears anomalous — verify earnings growth estimate; do NOT cite as primary valuation argument). FCF yield is -0.03% — negative, meaning the thesis depends entirely on future earnings materializing from the AI optics backlog filled through 2028. Nearest resistance at $942.16 (5.0% upside) vs. 26.6% downside to SMA50 produces 0.2:1 R:R. Bottom line: The AI photonics thesis is confirmed and legitimate, but at 36% above the 50-day average and with negative free cash flow, this is a stock to own at the right price — not this one.

PLTR.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 69x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.
PLTR.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Palantir has dropped 15.5% in a month and 13.7% over 5 days, with RSI at 34.1 — approaching oversold territory. The entry block is a soft CAUTION (not hard block, caution_source=base_scorer) due to forward PE of 68.8x (>40x threshold). Per TheStreet, President Trump endorsed PLTR on Truth Social on April 10 — the first sitting US president to publicly endorse a stock by ticker; while notable for sentiment, this is not a fundamental catalyst and does not change the valuation math. The defense AI thesis remains: the $10B Army IDIQ framework ceiling (not firm revenue) and the $448M Navy ShipOS deal per DoD press release are real contract vehicles, and 2026 revenue guidance of $7.18-7.20B (+61% YoY) per Rosenblatt/Daiwa notes is compelling growth. Risk/reward: invalidation at $126.30 (nearest support, 1.4% below) — tight stop warning: normal intraday volatility may trigger invalidation before the thesis is tested. Use $119.17 (6.9% below) as the mental stop for position sizing. Upside target $134.39 (4.9% above) gives 3.5:1 R:R with observed quality. The tight invalidation means the R:R looks good on paper but requires precise execution. PEG of 2.53 and forward PE of 68.8x are expensive even for 70% revenue growth — EV/EBITDA of 207.9x is extreme. Volume is high, confirming selling pressure. Earnings May 5 (22 days). Bottom line: Palantir's defense AI story is real and RSI is getting interesting, but at 69x forward earnings you're paying a premium that requires everything to go right — wait for RSI below 30 or a hold of $119 support with volume reversal before entering.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

WRD.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at WATCH. WeRide sits 3.78% above its 50-day SMA of $7.23 with RSI at 52.6 — slightly extended but within the watchable zone. The Singapore launch milestone is real: WeRide and Grab launched public autonomous ride operations in Punggol in early April 2026, with 30,000km of testing logged and commercial rollout planned mid-2026 per Simply Wall St. R:R of 2.9:1 is attractive — $7.23 invalidation (3.6% below) vs. $8.29 upside target (10.5% above) with observed R:R quality. Invalidation is the 50-day SMA; structural support at $6.85 (8.7% below) is the next meaningful level. Volume is below average — wait for confirmation before entering. The ADR/China delisting risk is a standing thesis invalidator — always a background risk for US-listed Chinese ADRs. Revenue growth of 123% is impressive for a pre-profit company, but burn rate alongside revenue is the key metric to monitor. No traditional valuation metrics (no PE, no FCF yield) — this is a speculative catalyst-driven name evaluated purely on commercial traction (Punggol fleet scale, geographic expansion to 8 countries, Uber/Grab partnerships). Earnings May 21 (38 days). Bottom line: WeRide's Singapore launch is a genuine commercial milestone for an autonomous vehicle company — but with thin volume and ADR delisting risk, this is a small starter position only if price holds above $7.23 with improving volume.

singapore.summary: All three Singapore banks remain range-bound or slightly extended with US10Y at 4.32% — still above the 4.20% NIM reset threshold. Monday's MAS Monetary Policy Statement is the single most important near-term catalyst for the cluster; a dovish signal could unlock the reset conditions (US10Y <4.20% + STI >5000) that have been unmet for 34 days. OCBC is the only name showing fundamental momentum (6.5% revenue growth, analyst upgrade, SGD 600M fund inflow), but its RSI block makes it the least actionable of the three.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally impaired — NIM compression ongoing, MAS catalyst imminent
singapore.key_development: MAS Monetary Policy Statement due April 14 is binary for the cluster — a surprise dovish pivot could shift all three from HOLD/WATCH/CAUTION toward actionable entries within 48 hours.

semiconductors.summary: SOXX surged to $386.60 (+2.23%) on Friday, extending the recovery from the 2026 lows — but most individual names are hard-blocked from entry due to extension. The cluster is bifurcating: TSMC and ASML have imminent earnings (April 15-16) that will either validate or puncture the AI demand narrative; NVDA, AMD, AVGO, and INTC are all 14-32% above their 50-day averages with overbought RSIs, making them observation-only. MU is the closest to a clean setup but blocked by the momentum rule after a 14.8% 5-day surge.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Earnings binary — ASML (April 15) and TSM (April 16) are the cluster's near-term pivots
semiconductors.key_development: TSMC Q1 2026 preliminary results showed 35% year-on-year revenue growth per Simply Wall St. — if the full earnings print April 16 confirms margins and the CoWoS capacity outlook, it could unlock WATCH or BUY signals across the semis cluster.

big_tech.summary: BigTech is split three ways: AMZN and AVGO are technically overbought after violent recoveries (11.7% and 18.1% above SMA50 respectively); GOOG is at a cleaner but slightly extended WATCH setup; MSFT is the contrarian candidate, now below all key moving averages and approaching an RSI where value investors start to look. The Monday Iran blockade news will test whether last week's risk-on momentum can hold against a fresh geopolitical shock.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — GOOG closest to entry, MSFT approaching contrarian zone, AMZN/AVGO extended
big_tech.key_development: Amazon's annual shareholder letter disclosed a $20B+ AI chip revenue run rate from Graviton/Trainium with triple-digit growth, and the company is evaluating selling chips externally — a potential new profit stream that justifies the AWS capex narrative.

interconnected.name: Coherent Corp
interconnected.reason: Received matching $2B NVDA strategic investment (per NVIDIA IR, March 2 2026) alongside LITE — the co-packaged optics thesis applies equally, and LITE's 36% extension above SMA50 makes COHR the more actionable photonics proxy for the NVDA AI interconnect thesis.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if COHR pulls back 8-10% from current levels or after NVDA's next demand signal confirms the optics backlog is holding.

active_risks: US-Iran peace talks collapsed over the weekend; US Navy will blockade Iranian ports starting Monday, per Yahoo Finance and Investor's Business Daily — Hormuz crisis enters active re-escalation phase (Day 36+)
Strait of Hormuz remains the critical chokepoint — ~20% of global oil transits it; East-west shipping rerouting adding 10-14 days to transit times per Bertling logistics update

US-China tactical tariff truce active: 24% reciprocal duties suspended until November 10, 2026; overall rate reduced from 41% to 31% per Coface analysis — semiconductor and electronics sectors remain exposed to future escalation
Taiwan geopolitical risk: TSMC's foundry dominance creates concentration risk in a contested geography — priced into positioning but not into signals
geopolitical.market_impact: WTI's 8.64% Friday surge to $104.91 and the confirmed US Navy blockade announcement will likely push oil higher at Monday's open, maintaining elevated energy costs that compress margins across logistics-dependent names and keep US10Y elevated above the 4.20% NIM reset threshold — directly blocking D05, O39, and U11 from their WATCH upgrade conditions. PLTR's defense AI contract momentum (Army IDIQ framework, Navy ShipOS deal) benefits from sustained geopolitical escalation, though the stock's 15.5% monthly decline shows the market is more focused on the 69x forward PE than the defense tailwind.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: US-Iran peace talks formally collapsed and the US Navy announced a blockade of Iranian ports starting Monday — a material escalation from the 'ceasefire fragile but holding' narrative of last week. This directly reverses the de-escalation thesis that had driven VIX down to 19.23 on Friday.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: No new entries Monday open — wait for the market to price the blockade escalation before assessing which names have overreacted; ASML and TSM earnings this week are the primary signal resets to watch.

events_this_week.event: MAS Monetary Policy Statement (April) — Singapore Monetary Authority rate/policy decision
US Navy Iran blockade begins — market open expected to gap lower on WTI spike and risk-off; monitor VIX for direction
